articles.title: Prediction Markets Shouldn’t Be So Easy to Play
articles.summary: Current prediction markets let basically anyone throw money at outcomes without much friction, but that accessibility might be undermining their core function as information aggregators. When barriers to entry are too low, you get noise traders drowning out informed participants, which means worse prices and less reliable signals about what's actually going to happen.

articles.title: The prediction market backlash has arrived
articles.summary: Critics are finally pushing back against the prediction market hype, questioning their accuracy and influence on everything from elections to policy decisions. The backlash threatens to slow mainstream adoption just as platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi were gaining serious institutional credibility and regulatory wins.

articles.title: The Kalshi and Polymarket CEO feud: They hate each other
articles.summary: The CEOs of America's two biggest prediction market platforms are publicly feuding, turning what should be a growing industry into a personal grudge match. This executive drama matters because when the heads of Kalshi and Polymarket are busy throwing punches instead of building better products, it gives traditional betting companies and regulators more ammunition to dismiss prediction markets as immature toys rather than serious financial instruments.

articles.title: The History of Prediction Markets — and What It Tells Us Today
articles.summary: Prediction markets have a surprisingly rich history that stretches back centuries, from medieval commodity futures to modern-day political betting platforms. Understanding how these markets evolved reveals why they work so well at aggregating information and why they keep bouncing back despite regulatory crackdowns.

articles.title: Pa. lawmaker proposes regulating prediction markets in Pennsylvania
articles.summary: A Pennsylvania legislator wants to create regulatory framework for prediction markets in the state. This could open up another major US market for platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket, potentially adding millions more users to the prediction economy.

articles.title: The Wildest Frat Party on Campus? Prediction Markets
articles.summary: College campuses are becoming unlikely hotbeds for prediction market trading, with students treating forecasting like the latest social craze. This matters because young, risk-tolerant demographics with disposable time often drive adoption of new financial products before they hit mainstream markets.

articles.title: MrBeast's company fired a video editor after Kalshi accused the employee of insider trading
articles.summary: MrBeast's production company terminated a video editor following allegations from prediction market Kalshi that the employee engaged in insider trading. The incident highlights how even entertainment industry workers now have access to material non-public information that can move markets, potentially forcing platforms to expand their monitoring beyond traditional finance sectors.

articles.title: A Technology for a Low-Trust Society
articles.summary: Prediction markets are emerging as the go-to solution for navigating a world where traditional institutions have lost credibility and information sources can't be trusted. When people stop believing experts and media, they'll bet real money on outcomes, creating more reliable signals than any poll or pundit ever could.

articles.title: Kalshi faces backlash over handling of $54M market on Khamenei’s ouster
articles.summary: Kalshi is getting hammered by users after controversial decisions around its $54M market betting on whether Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei gets ousted from power. The blowback highlights how platform operators can torch user trust when they fumble high-stakes geopolitical markets, especially ones where resolution criteria get murky fast.

articles.title: Michigan sues Kalshi, alleges app is skirting state's gambling laws
articles.summary: Michigan's attorney general is going after Kalshi, claiming the prediction market platform violates the state's gambling regulations. This could set a dangerous precedent for other states looking to crack down on prediction markets, potentially forcing platforms to geofence entire states or face costly legal battles.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Let You Bet on Anything, but This AI Stock Is the Surest Bet I've Seen
articles.summary: A prediction market bettor claims they've found the most reliable AI stock play available for wagering. The call matters because AI stocks have been driving massive betting volume across platforms, and if someone's cracked the code on consistent winners, other bettors will pile in fast.

articles.title: Understanding prediction markets with Louis Pellegrino and Charles Farrell
articles.summary: Louis Pellegrino and Charles Farrell break down how prediction markets actually work, from basic mechanics to advanced trading strategies. Smart money follows the insights of experienced traders who've learned to spot inefficiencies and capitalize on crowd psychology in these markets.
